steps.title: Find the slope of line s
steps.content: Rewrite $6x+2y=0$ in slope-intercept form: $$2y=-6x$$ $$y=-3x.$$ So line $s$ has slope $-3$.

steps.title: Use the perpendicular slope
steps.content: A line perpendicular to slope $-3$ has slope equal to the negative reciprocal, which is $\frac{1}{3}$. Line $t$ passes through $(5,0)$, so its equation is $$y=\frac{1}{3}(x-5).$$

steps.title: Use the given y-value
steps.content: The other point on line $t$ is $(k,\frac{4}{3})$, so plug in $y=\frac{4}{3}$: $$\frac{4}{3}=\frac{1}{3}(k-5).$$ Multiply by $3$ to get $4=k-5$, so $k=9$.

steps.title: Check the point
steps.content: At $x=9$, the line $y=\frac{1}{3}(x-5)$ gives $y=\frac{1}{3}(4)=\frac{4}{3}$. Therefore the point is $(9,\frac{4}{3})$, and the value of $k$ is 9.
